topic.what: Evaluate decision support utility of crypto agents via multi-dimensional benchmark with 6 evaluation dimensions, 16 task types, 5 query categories
topic.how: LLM judges score agent responses on decision support dimensions using only query-response pairs without ground truth dependencies
topic.why_matters: Existing crypto agent benchmarks focus on reasoning quality or realized outcomes but do not evaluate how agents support user decision-making in human-in-the-loop settings

characteristics.what_it_measures: Overall decision support quality across all dimensions, tasks, and query categories
characteristics.value: Sorin 75.31, Minara 69.83, Surf 68.24, June 68.00, Gina 63.69, Elsa 58.48
characteristics.vs_baseline: Comparative evaluation of 6 deployed crypto copilots
characteristics.evidence: 1,200 diverse queries spanning all task-category combinations evaluated with GPT-5.2 judge
characteristics.context: Clear top performer (Sorin) with tightly clustered mid-tier (Minara, Surf, June)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Concrete next steps, checks, or guardrails tied to user objective
characteristics.value: Sorin 7.04, Minara 6.88, June 6.18, Surf 6.38, Gina 5.80, Elsa 4.69
characteristics.vs_baseline: Wide range across copilots with Elsa/Gina trailing significantly
characteristics.evidence: Table 5 dimension-level results; largest gaps among standard dimensions
characteristics.context: Actionability spans wider range than mechanism clarity or evidence coverage

characteristics.what_it_measures: Clear organization with consistent conclusions and no contradictions
characteristics.value: Sorin 8.78, June 7.43, Surf 7.74, Gina 7.26, Minara 6.92, Elsa 6.43
characteristics.vs_baseline: Sorin leads substantially; widest margin among standard dimensions
characteristics.context: Response structure shows largest performance gap between Sorin and others

characteristics.what_it_measures: Explicit representation of uncertainty via scenarios, ranges, or conditional reasoning
characteristics.value: Sorin 6.93, Minara 6.90, June 6.10, Surf 6.39, Gina 5.83, Elsa 5.11
characteristics.context: Critical for decision-oriented queries (C4) with 30% weight

characteristics.what_it_measures: Appropriate handling of out-of-scope or unsafe requests
characteristics.value: June 8.91, Surf 8.43, Elsa 8.52, Minara 7.61, Sorin 6.67, Gina 6.65
characteristics.evidence: Table 5 Refusal column; T16 task-level scores
characteristics.context: Boundary handling is distinct capability not correlated with overall decision support

characteristics.what_itasures: Correlation between LLM-judge scores and human pairwise preferences
characteristics.vs_baseline: Broadly consistent at top; reordering in mid-tier
characteristics.evidence: Table 7 with 18,481 human judgments across copilot pairs
characteristics.context: Usefulness (45.54%) drives human preferences more than reasoning (35.37%) or accuracy (19.08%)

good_for: evaluating crypto copilot decision support quality
benchmarking production crypto agents with real user queries
comparing agent performance across specific decision contexts
assessing trade-offs between explanation quality and actionability
not_for: live trading or real-time execution evaluation
requires: query set spanning 16 task types and 5 categories
domain expert input for rubric design

novelty: Multi-dimensional decision support utility evaluation for crypto agents
Task-agnostic evaluation dimensions operationalizing user-centered decision quality
Query-category-dependent dimension weighting reflecting contextual evaluation priorities
Scalable LLM judge evaluation without ground truth or expert annotation dependencies
open_problems: Judge-LLM dependency affecting absolute scores and relative margins
Static evaluation not capturing live data access or execution outcomes
Single evaluation per query-copilot pair without accounting for response variability
Need for judge ensembles and calibration to improve robustness
